The claim that 4x collocation would proportionally reduce this error is speculative and unsupported by ablation or theory."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should I focus on the technical and logical soundness of the claims (e.g., validity of Proposition 1, experiment-to-claim scope alignment), or primarily on prose and writing quality?"}

rationale.R1.2: Proposition 1 conflates optimizer convergence with solution accuracy: driving L_pde below epsilon does not imply O(epsilon) proximity to the true solution without invoking stability or coercivity conditions (e.g., inf-sup, Lax-Milgram) not stated in the excerpt; the sweeping central claim that the method works for any Reynolds number admitting a unique weak solution has no formal grounding here.
rationale.R1.3: Experiments cover Re=1000 and Re=5000, both below the paper's stated target regime of Re>=1e4; only Re=20000 falls inside the claimed scope, yet that case shows 19.4% error. The claim that 4x collocation would proportionally reduce this error is speculative and unsupported by ablation or theory.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on the technical and logical soundness of the claims (e.g., validity of Proposition 1, experiment-to-claim scope alignment), or primarily on prose and writing quality?
